title: Organ-on-a-Chip (OoC) Technology
focus: Micro-scale 3D culture systems mimicking human organs for drug testing and disease modeling.
rationale: A distinct biotechnology tool with ethical and cost-effective benefits for drug development, representing a shift towards New Approach Methods (NAMs).
sectionExcerpt: Organ-on-a-chip (OoC) refers to a micro-scale system used for mimicking the human body environment. It is a human-relevant 3D culture model (New Approach Methods). Cells grow into 3D structures on a chip using a polymer resembling real tissue.
evidenceBullets: OoC is a micro-scale system mimicking the human body environment.
It is also known as 'New Approach Methods' (NAMs) and uses 3D culture models.
Provides an ethical, cost-effective platform for accurate drug testing and personalized medicine.

title: RNA Silencing and microRNA
focus: Gene regulation mechanisms including TGS, PTGS/RNAi, and the role of small RNAs (siRNA, miRNA) in suppressing gene expression.
rationale: Core genetics concept with applications in agriculture (e.g., protecting bananas from CMV) and cellular regulation, highly testable for UPSC.
sectionExcerpt: RNA silencing is a gene regulation mechanism that reduces gene expression by suppressing transcription (TGS) or degrading specific RNA after transcription (PTGS/RNAi). It involves small RNA molecules like siRNA or miRNA binding to mRNA to destroy it or block translation.
evidenceBullets: RNA silencing reduces gene expression via transcriptional gene silencing (TGS) or posttranscriptional gene silencing (PTGS/RNAi).
Techniques like Host-Induced Gene Silencing (HIGS) and Spray-Induced Gene Silencing (SIGS) strengthen plant immunity.
microRNA (miRNA) is a small non-coding RNA that controls gene expression by binding with mRNA in the cytoplasm to prevent translation or degrade it.

title: Pangenome and HiFi Sequencing
focus: The concept of a pangenome, its creation using PacBio HiFi sequencing, and its applications in agriculture and precision medicine.
rationale: Important genomics advancement with recent applications in Asian rice and human precision medicine.
sectionExcerpt: A Pangenome is a collection of genome sequences from many individuals of the same species. Chinese scientists created the first Pangenome of Asian rice using 'PacBio high-fidelity' (HiFi) sequencing, a single-molecule, real-time (SMRT) long-read sequencing method.
evidenceBullets: A Pangenome is a collection of genome sequences from many individuals of the same species.
PacBio HiFi sequencing is a single-molecule, real-time (SMRT) long-read sequencing method.
Pangenomes help introduce disease-tolerance traits in crops and identify population-specific genetic markers in humans.

title: SHUKR Gene
focus: Discovery of the SHUKR gene by CSIR-CCMB and its role in flowering plant evolution.
rationale: Specific, highly testable factual discovery related to plant genetics and evolutionary biology.
sectionExcerpt: Researchers from CSIR-CCMB discovered the SHUKR gene, which controls F-box genes to manage proteins for healthy pollen development. It helps explain Darwin's abominable mystery regarding the sudden rise of flowering plants.
evidenceBullets: The SHUKR gene was researched by CSIR-CCMB, Hyderabad.
It controls F-box genes involved in developing healthy pollen.
Its fast evolution helps explain Darwin's 'abominable mystery' about the sudden rise of flowering plants.

title: DNA Profiling (DNA Fingerprinting)
focus: Techniques and markers used in DNA profiling, including VNTRs, STRs, mtDNA, Y chromosome, and SNPs.
rationale: Fundamental forensic biotechnology topic with multiple specific methods (STR, mtDNA, SNPs) that are highly examinable.
sectionExcerpt: DNA Profiling identifies individuals by analyzing unique patterns in their DNA, based on the variability of noncoding regions called VNTRs. Key methods include Short Tandem Repeats (STR), Mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) for degraded nuclear DNA, Y chromosome STRs for paternal lineage, and Single Nucleotide Polymorphisms (SNPs).
evidenceBullets: DNA profiling is based on variability in noncoding regions of DNA called Variable Number of Tandem Repeats (VNTRs).
Mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) is used when nuclear DNA is degraded.
Y chromosome profiling focuses on STRs inherited from father to son.
Single Nucleotide Polymorphisms (SNPs) are single-base differences unique to individuals.

title: Jumping Genes (Transposable Elements)
focus: Characteristics and mechanisms of transposable elements (jumping genes) like LINE-1.
rationale: Core genetics concept explaining genome mutability and composition, recently in news due to LINE-1 and TnpB genome editing.
sectionExcerpt: Jumping Genes, or Transposable Elements, are DNA sequences that move across the genome, affecting gene expression by cutting and pasting or copying themselves. They can cause mutations and make up more than 50% of the human genome.
evidenceBullets: Jumping genes (transposable elements) are DNA sequences that move from one location to another on the genome.
They move by either physically cutting and pasting or copying themselves to new locations.
More than 50% of the human genome is composed of transposable elements.
LINE-1 is a jumping gene that binds to cellular DNA during brief periods of cell division.

title: CAR-T Cell Therapy (NexCAR19)
focus: India's first indigenous CAR T-cell therapy for B-cell blood cancers.
rationale: Major indigenous biotechnology achievement in cancer treatment, highly relevant for current affairs in science and tech.
sectionExcerpt: NexCAR19 is India's first indigenous Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-cell (CAR-T) therapy. It was developed for B-cell blood cancers by ImmunoACT, supported by DBT and BIRAC.
evidenceBullets: NexCAR19 is India's first indigenous CAR T-cell therapy.
It is developed specifically for B-cell blood cancers.
Developed by ImmunoACT (incubated under IIT Bombay and Tata Memorial Hospital) with support from DBT and BIRAC.
